raw.objective: “To determine the effects of CYP3A5 polymorphism on CBZ pharmacokinetic parameters when CBZ was used either as monotherapy or co-administered with PHT, PB or VPA”
raw.covariates: Tested: CYP3A5, WT, BMI Included: None
raw.observations: No statistical significance found for any covariates, possibly due to simplification of PK
raw.structure: Simplified 1 compartment with no absorption or volume
raw.equations: Css-avg=(Dose*S)/(Tau*CL/F) (CL/F = S*Dose/Tau*Css-avg) S=Salt factor Css-avg= average steady state concentration Tau=dosing interval (hours)

extraction_target.title: Effect of CYP3A5 genotypes on the pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine when used as monotherapy or co-administered with phenytoin, phenobarbital or valproic acid in Thai patients.

structural_model.description: Simplified 1 compartment with no absorption or volume

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “To determine the effects of CYP3A5 polymorphism on CBZ pharmacokinetic parameters when CBZ was used either as monotherapy or co-administered with PHT, PB or VPA”

covariates.raw: Tested: CYP3A5, WT, BMI Included: None

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
